channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Resident Evil Requiem Becomes Fastest-Selling Game In Franchise History
item.content:encoded:
The good people still love Resident Evil as much as ever in 2026. It turns out that Capcom’s arduous marketing campaign for the game has been a success, as Resident Evil Requiem has already sold over 5 million copies in a ridiculously short space of time.

Resident Evil Requiem has topped Metacritic’s greatest games ever list in terms of user score—and it’s hard to explain how unbelievable this is when you consider this includes ALL games.

The game has gone one better and become the series’ fastest-selling title, too, beating out all the other legendary games in the franchise.

Resident Evil Requiem Sells Over 5 Million Copies In 5 Days

An official Capcom press release from March 4 shows that Resident Evil Requiem “Surpasses 5 Million Units!”

Capcom also added in the press release that the franchise (as of December 31, 2025) has sold over 183 million units, meaning Requiem takes its total closer to 190 million, and it’s likely to surpass 200 million by the end of 2026.

Additionally, renowned leaker Dusk Golem claims that this “makes it the top-selling first week of a RE game ever, overtaking the previous record holder.”

The previous record holder was Resident Evil 6, which clocked in at 4.5 million copies sold in one week, whereas Requiem has cleared 5 million in only five days!

With rumors about Resident Evil Requiem DLC and expansions abound, this might only be the beginning for the terrifying latest installment.

A round of applause is necessary for Resident Evil Requiem.

Have you had a chance to play it yet? Is it worthy of all the praise it’s had so far? Share your thoughts and opinions through the free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Resi 9 news and content, check out how to solve The Final Puzzle in Resident Evil Requiem, how to crack the Roulette Easter Egg, and if the fan-favorite Hunk appears in Requiem.

MOBILE APP

item.title: How Do Marathon Wipes Work? What You Keep & Lose, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Bungie has confirmed that Marathon will have regular wipes throughout the extraction shooter’s lifecycle, and raised a lot of questions; namely, how often will they happen, and what do you get to keep or lose?

Wipes are fairly standard for extraction shooters, with the likes of Escape from Tarkov and ARC Raiders both offering their own renditions of wipes, but there isn’t really a standard method for fans to expect.

Well, details have finally been shared, and if you’re confused about how wipes will work in Marathon, then you’re in the right place, as Insider Gaming has it all explained for you down below.

How Often Will Wipes Occur In Marathon?

You can expect a wipe to occur every three months in Marathon, as they’ll happen alongside the beginning of a brand new Season.

That makes them Seasonal wipes, and that’s a fairly standard affair for extraction shooters. Bungie has clarified that they intend for everyone to go into new Seasons back on the same playing field, with the same sense of threat of death in a world that’s meant to constantly evolve around you.

  • More Insider Gaming: Marathon 2026 Roadmap

And unlike ARC Raiders, you can’t opt in or out of a wipe. Instead, you’re forced to partake, so you’ll need to raise your power floor every Season if you wish to remain at the top. Still, it should create a fun final week of each Season, where Runners go into Zones without a care in the world, and it’s the perfect time to use your best weapons without any sense of gear fear.

What Do You Keep & Lose With Every Seasonal Marathon Wipe?

With every Seasonal wipe in Marathon, you’ll lose practically everything. That includes all collected gear, purchased upgrades, and Faction Ranks.

  • More Insider Gaming: Marathon Season 1 Release Date Countdown

It will truly be a fresh start every Season, as you’ll need to start from the bottom and work your way back up to an elite Runner capable of completing endgame activities. In fact, it’s probably easier to list what you will keep, as you’ll lose pretty much everything else; with that in mind, here’s what remains with every Seasonal Marathon wipe:

  • Cosmetics
  • Achievements
  • Codex Progression
  • Liaison Contract

That means that whilst you’ll have to rank up all six of the Factions every Season, you won’t have to unlock them again, which is a handy start at least. Still, that means that every Season there’s bound to be plenty for you to work through, alongside all the new content that arrives with each new Season.

How do you feel about Marathon wipes? Are you glad that they’re compulsory? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Marathon content, check out every Runner Shell, as well as when Ranked is coming to Marathon.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Does Monster Hunter Stories 3 Have Early Access? Demo Status, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Some games are just too difficult to wait for, and any form of early access is worth chasing. Monster Hunter Stories 3 might just be one of those games, as the third entry into the series is generating a huge amount of hype throughout the fanbase.

However, not every game offers early access; typically, it’s offered as a reward for pre-ordering or as an add-on for a special edition of the game. Monster Hunter Stories 3 does have numerous editions available for purchase, but do any of them offer early access?

If you want to jump into Monster Hunter Stories 3 early, carry on reading to find out if you can, or if there are any alternatives instead.

Can You Play Monster Hunter Stories 3 Through Early Access?

Unfortunately, there is no early access on offer for Monster Hunter Stories 3, meaning you’ll have to wait until the full release to jump into the adventure.

It’s not entirely surprising, though, as Capcom rarely offer early access for its titles. Instead, it’s the norm for fans to all jump into the adventure together at the same time, with the tradition seemingly carried on for Monster Hunter Stories 3.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is Monster Hunter Stories 3 On Xbox Game Pass?

However, don’t lose all hope. While you might not be able to get your hands on the full game earlier than the rest, you can certainly give it a go before release with the demo…

Does Monster Hunter Stories 3 Have A Demo?

Yes, Monster Hunter Stories 3 does have a playable demo, and it’s downloadable on all platforms it will release on.

It’s not just any demo, either, as it’s a remarkably sizable one at that. In the demo, you’ll gain access to the early portion of the game, and it won’t be a waste of time, as you can transfer your save data to the full version upon release.

  • More Insider Gaming: Monster Hunter Stories 3 Release Date Countdown

Whilst details for it are slightly scarce, if you take a look at some Steam reviews, you can get a clearer picture of how much content is on offer. Most users report having around 10-12 hours of playtime, with one user reporting a whopping 75 hours!

Whilst that’s assuredly an extreme example, there’s undoubtedly plenty of hours’ worth of content available, so if you’re desperate for a taste of what Monster Hunter Stories 3 has to offer, give the demo a go!

Are you excited to play Monster Hunter Stories 3? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming, check out whether Crimson Desert has early access, as well as the Marathon 2026 Roadmap.
